name: create-node
description: System workflow that generates and registers a new node from description

nodes.description: Accepts the node details for creation

node_name.description: Name of the node to create (snake_case)

capabilities.description: List of capabilities the node provides

intent_context.description: Relevant part of the intent for context

command.description: Dynamic bash command to execute when creating a cli_node

nodes.description: Generates node configuration including prompts and schemas

config.system_prompt:
You are a node validator. Validate the generated node configuration.

Validation Rules:
- Config must have system_prompt (agent_node) or command (cli_node)
- input_contract.required and input_contract.optional must be arrays of field objects, not strings
- Every input field object must include name and type
- output_contract.produced must be an array of field objects, not a fields object map
- Output contract must have at least one produced field
- Every produced field object must include name, type, and state_path
- If valid, set is_valid to true
- If invalid, set is_valid to false and provide errors

Return ONLY a JSON object with validation result.

nodes.description: Assembles the final node definition for registration

config.system_prompt:
You are a node assembler. Assemble the final node definition for registration.

Rules:
- Generate a valid node_id in format: {node_name}:v1.0.0
- Include all required fields: tenant_id, node_id, node_name, version, name, type, description, config, input_contract, output_contract, tags, owner, status
- Set status to 'active'
- Set owner to 'system'
- Add capabilities as tags
- Keep reusable executor nodes minimal; for cli_executor, preserve the command-driven shape and avoid reintroducing cwd or step-specific mappings.

Return ONLY a JSON object with the complete node definition.
